page: Microsoft Azure E32_v4 is an Ev4-family virtual machine configured with 32 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of system memory. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it utilizes a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. The hardware allocation consists of 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The processor features a cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 35 MB L3 cache. It supports instruction set extensions including AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI, but lacks AMX and SHA_NI support. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark results position the E32_v4 primarily in the average performance tier across most compute and memory metrics. The Passmark memory score is a notable exception, ranking in the strong tier at 2782.12. In contrast, single-threaded data processing shows some limitations, with weak ratings in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. Multi-core performance is stable, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 29786.22 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 10522.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 105.38 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 47.58 GB/sec for large block write operations. Large-scale LLM inference is a clear bottleneck, with the 70B model prompt processing rated as poor at 4.54 tokens/sec.

With a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the E32_v4 offers high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-performance local storage. The absence of local NVMe SSDs means all storage operations rely on network-attached disks, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. Given the average database operations score of 10873.4 and a Redis SET throughput of 27.42 million operations per second, this server is suited for relational databases, in-memory caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring a large memory footprint without intensive local disk I/O.
description: The Microsoft Azure E32_v4 is an Ev4-family virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high-density 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz across 16 physical cores, it supports AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI instructions. This instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Performance benchmarks place the E32_v4 in the average tier for most compute and memory operations, though it achieves a strong Passmark memory score. Single-threaded compression tasks represent a performance bottleneck, showing weak results in gzip and bzip3 tests. Given its high memory capacity and average database operations performance, the E32_v4 is suited for memory-intensive workloads, including relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications that do not require local NVMe storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32_v4 is an Ev4-family virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor, designed for memory-intensive workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, lacking local storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and Intel Xeon processors for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 256.0 GB RAM providing a high-density 1:8 memory ratio
Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with AVX-512 instruction support
No local storage or bundled GPUs included
Suited for relational databases and in-memory caching
